artifacts.why_it_exists: Human-readable starting point for reviewing the artifact pack.
Machine-readable index describing every artifact and its score semantics.
Shows the strict inference stdout contract using only [START], [STEP], and [END].

artifacts.score_note: This full-episode score should match the cautious_policy hard benchmark score (0.9350).
artifacts.why_it_exists: Full hard-task run from the cautious policy used in the benchmark comparison.

artifacts.score_note: This is a single-step grader score, not an episode score.
artifacts.why_it_exists: Representative final rollback decision from the cautious hard-task run.

artifacts.score_note: This is a hard-specific illustrative rollout example. It is not the same object as the multi-task `aggressive_policy` comparator in the benchmark results.
artifacts.why_it_exists: Full hard-task aggressive rollout example that promotes through the hard watch window.

artifacts.score_note: This is the risky watch-window promotion step, not the full-episode score.
artifacts.why_it_exists: Captures the aggressive rollout example's signature mistake: promotion during the hard watch window.

artifacts.score_note: This is an in-progress episode score through the watch-window hold, not a completed benchmark score.
artifacts.why_it_exists: Shows the hard-task watch window after the hold decision has been taken.

artifacts.score_note: This is a single-step grader score for the hold decision on the hard watch window.
artifacts.why_it_exists: Isolates the watch-window hold decision that makes the hard task discriminative.

artifacts.score_note: This is a completed expert-task episode from the cautious policy.
artifacts.why_it_exists: Full expert-task run showing that the cautious policy ignores the phantom alert and later rolls back on the real differential breach.

artifacts.score_note: This is the phantom-alert hold decision score, not a full-episode score.
artifacts.why_it_exists: Single-step proof that the phantom alert is rewarded when the agent verifies metrics instead of rolling back.

artifacts.score_note: This is a completed recovery-task episode from the cautious policy.
artifacts.why_it_exists: Full recovery-task run showing that the cautious policy holds through a transient canary-only degradation and then continues the rollout safely.

artifacts.score_note: This is the transient-recovery hold decision score, not a full-episode score.
artifacts.why_it_exists: Single-step proof that a transient canary-only degradation is rewarded as a hold, not a rollback.
Compares shallow baseline, cautious policy, and aggressive policy across all tasks.

artifacts.score_note: Aggregate scores are the mean completed-episode scores across the documented benchmark seeds.
artifacts.why_it_exists: Shows that the main policy ordering remains coherent across deterministic non-zero seeds, not just the canonical seed=0 profiles.
Enumerates event ordering and signal-shape differences across deterministic seeds so reviewers can inspect task-family breadth directly.

artifacts.score_note: These are validator compatibility proofs, not benchmark scores.
artifacts.why_it_exists: Captures structural OpenEnv validation and live local-server validation from the generated artifact run.
Shows representative validator-safe endpoints plus the recommended /episodes HTTP flow.
Short benchmark-level summary of what the artifact pack proves.

score_semantics.episode_score: Normalized running average across all decisions taken so far in an episode.
score_semantics.grader_total_score: Normalized score for exactly one decision under POST /grader.
score_semantics.partial_episode_note: The watch-window transcript is intentionally in_progress. Its episode_score is a running average through that point, not a completed-policy benchmark average.
score_semantics.policy_benchmark_score: Final episode_score for a named policy on a named task.
